chapters.title: Dreams need a positive attitude behind them
chapters.text:
We often face decisions that can change life direction. Fear of the unknown and attitude toward the problem make choices difficult.

After ten years practicing law, Jeff Keller quit to focus on motivational speaking full time — a drastic move that surprised people and seemed impossible. Using strategies in this book, he made a successful transition and found that positive attitude solves more than career questions.

Changes can be scary at first, yet they should motivate you to do what it takes to win.

A bad attitude is like a flat tire: you cannot move until you change it. Keep reading to learn how positive ideas produce breakthroughs.

A positive attitude is a person's passport to a better tomorrow. ~ Unknown

chapters.title: Attitude shapes achievement and influence
chapters.text:
Your attitude is the mental filter through which you experience the world. But because biology and experience make people different, not everyone can see the world the same way. Some people choose to be optimistic about different situations, while others want to dwell on the negatives.

You can't always control circumstances. But you can control your thoughts. ~ Charles Popplestone

Many times at the start of a task or an adventure, we are filled with optimism and a desire to make a change, but as time goes on, we get discouraged by criticism, rejection, disappointments or even doubts. The longer we leave these negative emotions bottled in, the more it affects our ability to stay positive. As you encounter difficult situations, you must take time off to carefully detach yourself from the negative occurrences that make you bitter and dissatisfied so that you can see the world in a different light. Your attitude is your window to the world, and it affects everything in your life.

Nobody else can do the job of cleaning your window when it gets dirty because it is always up to you to make the changes in your life. Yes, you might have had some bad experiences in the past, but it is no excuse. No one has an excuse to have a negative attitude.

Having a positive attitude is necessary to activate other principles of success because everything depends on your attitude.

chapters.title: Positive thinking must go deeper than mood
chapters.text:
Writers, philosophers, and religious leaders agree: thoughts determine actions. Think about a goal and you take steps toward it; chances are you achieve it.

What we sincerely believe regarding ourselves is true for us. ~ Orison Swett Marden

Spending little time thinking about a goal produces weak results; positive thinking must become lifestyle.

Relationships reflect how you think about yourself and what you think you deserve. Unchanged thoughts mean unchanged relationships.

Changing thought process takes time and repetition. Reading positive literature or listening to motivational audio trains the mind toward positive thoughts.

Success requires patience, effort, and commitment. Difficulty remains even with positive thoughts; belief and persistence overcome obstacles.

Past thoughts created the present; present thoughts shape the future.

chapters.title: Stop comparing — run your own race
chapters.text:
Celine Dion once said she pictured herself being this successful from the age of 5. Many successful people intentionally create an image of the success they want from a young age. You can do the same too; visualize yourself in any situation you want. But a lot of the time, we produce results that fall short of our potential because we do not envision a more successful version of ourselves.

While trying to create a mental picture of yourself, ensure you're not thinking of too many things at the same time. Look for a comfortable position so that your thoughts can flow freely.

You always have control over the pictures you allow into your mind, and your conscious decisions make all the difference. Always choose new pictures that fit in your life's plan. Also, feel free to change the interpretation of an event as you desire. Just like everything else that is worth a try, creating a positive mental picture takes time and requires patience and persistent focus. Comparing yourself to someone else only takes away the beauty of your journey toward a better mental picture. It also slows you down and prevents you from seeing all your potential. Changing your mental picture can translate to physical activities such as writing positive words in a card that you can read every day. But be careful not to feed your mind with negative words or visual aids no matter how harmless they look.

Imagination takes work to become a reality. So, after creating a mental picture, you need to put in the necessary work.

chapters.title: Commitment means staying when it gets hard
chapters.text:
Commitment is the willingness to do whatever it takes to accomplish your goals. No matter how difficult it gets, commitment means sticking to plans and not giving up. Persistence follows closely because you must follow through with relentless determination to see goals through.

Once you are committed to a goal, you begin to attract everything you need to accomplish it. Your mind becomes focused on finding things you were formerly blind to.

Do not set a rigid timeline for accomplishment. Instead, be open to changes and new experiences.

Many people shy away from projects because they don't have it all figured out. That is no reason to remain stagnant because when you have willingness to do whatever it takes, your mind attracts what you need.

Through obstacles, mistakes, and disappointments, commitment helps you realize they are only temporary and will go away.

Take David Baldacci as an example. He started as a lawyer but was committed to learning the craft of writing. He practiced every night from 10 p.m. to 2 a.m. for many years. After ten years of writing he still had no big breaks, yet he did not give up. He persisted, and in 1996 all his efforts paid off.

If you have a goal in mind but are not willing to go all out, or you decide that after a period of little progress you'll give up, you are not committed. Commitment means giving your best all the time and staying the course.

Mere interest does not get the job done; commitment does.

chapters.title: Focus on solutions, not the problem
chapters.text:
Complaining when problems arise is natural, yet after complaining you choose whether to keep drowning or make the best of it. Every coin has two sides; opportunity may hide behind problems. A rejection letter might push skill work or entrepreneurship; a health scare might change lifestyle. Whatever the problem, a flipside may exist.

When tragedy strikes, take time to face reality for deeper insight.

Disappointments become what you make them. They highlight important things, reveal hidden abilities, and from adversity to adversity courage and determination grow, changing how you handle life.

You are the sum of every experience you've ever passed through; good or bad.

chapters.title: Words reveal thought and shape outcomes
chapters.text:
Your words carry incredible power. They can ruin opportunities or change your life faster than you imagine because they reinforce beliefs, and beliefs create reality.

If you think you are not very good at your job, you begin saying it repeatedly to people around you. That strengthens the belief, leads to little or no action, and produces poor results at work.

This vicious cycle of negativity continues until you choose to change the words you speak to yourself and others. Negative words reinforce negative beliefs and encourage negative results.

To break free, give yourself as many positive talks as possible. Listen to yourself say the right things and let them take root in your subconscious mind. Then choose to tell only people who will support your effort because they help you stay accountable.

Stop repeating negative statements in your career, relationships, health, or finances.

Zig Ziglar decided to reduce his weight from 202 pounds to 165 pounds while writing "See You At The Top." He included that he successfully lost weight in the book and placed 25,000 copies, which meant he had to lose 37 pounds to maintain credibility. He succeeded because the words in that book held him accountable. The strategy can be risky, yet it is useful for very important goals.

The power of words is evident in daily interactions and in how we articulate emotions. You can control words by building a positive belief system that begins with awareness.

Be careful not to discuss your goals with negative people. All they'll do is point out reasons you won't be successful.

chapters.title: Attitude is free and available to everyone
chapters.text:
Almost everyone answers "How are you?" daily. The response reveals attitude. Answers can be negative, mediocre, or positive. Negative phrases like "I'm tired" or "I'm feeling terrible" are common.

Mediocre responses like "I'm okay" beat negative ones yet still diminish energy and produce mediocre results.

The slightest negative response can trigger reactions that ruin a day.

Positive responses like "I'm terrific" or "I feel great" improve self-feeling and encourage conversation.

Even when tired or terrible, choosing positivity does nothing to worsen the situation. We become what we think about.

Maintaining positive outlook can feel uncomfortable, yet practice makes it natural.

chapters.title: Be the person others want around
chapters.text:
Nobody enjoys it when someone else begins to chronicle their problems, but a lot of us still do it very often. It is a different thing when people discuss their issues because they're seeking solutions or share experiences with their family and friends to let them in on the activities in their lives. These are perfectly normal and can yield positive results.

The problem arises when a person decides to be graphic in explaining gory details of a problem, especially when the person listening can do nothing about this situation. This destroys everyone's good mood and leads to a game of proving who has it worse.

Complaining about the things that you cannot change not only wastes your time, but it also compounds the problem.

Life is full of many challenges; it is essential that you save your energy for the things that you can change. Being optimistic in the face of challenges makes you have a sense of what is important to you and helps you see situations as they are. Eddie Rickenbacker drifted on a log raft for 21 days because he was optimistic that someone would rescue him.

If you find yourself complaining, think about the things you are grateful for. Complaining not only accomplishes nothing but pushes people away from you, and reinforces your problems.

Take your time to discover things that you like and bring you the most peace; you'll be happier.

chapters.title: Surround yourself with nourishing people
chapters.text:
The world has toxic people and nourishing people. Toxic ones dwell on negatives, drag you into problems, and discourage new things. Nourishing people are supportive, positive, and enthusiastic.

The mind dwells on whatever it is fed repeatedly. Fill it with positive messages and interact with positive people.

Evaluate friendships. If you spend time with negative people, cut them off when possible; with family you cannot avoid, set limits on access.

If you want to be a high achiever, gravitate toward people who nourish your mind.

People you allow in life either choke dreams or inspire growth.

chapters.title: Break out of the comfort zone
chapters.text:
To achieve goals and develop potential, you must be willing to go the extra mile and do things you are afraid of. It is normal to fear big steps or new ideas because they move you out of the comfort zone — familiar behavior where you feel safe.

To be successful, face fear directly. Your fear might be public speaking, rejection, or starting a business, but believing you are stronger than those fears lets you overcome them.

If you want to be successful, you must be willing to be uncomfortable. ~ Gil Eagles

Much of the time we choose the easy path, backing away from fear for momentary relief from anxiety. That does not eliminate fear; it increases its power to limit growth. There is no magic wand to remove worry or make discomfort disappear, yet confronting situations with positive attitude is a bold step toward success.

There are no benefits from avoiding fears because they keep coming back.

Consider Dottie Burman: for thirty-two years she taught English in a New York high school, yet since age ten she wanted show business. She slowly wrote songs and performed as a hobby until she gathered courage to pursue performing full time. In 1988 she retired from teaching and launched a one-woman musical show about her fears of leaving a secure job. That became the start of a career releasing more songs and performing across the country. If she confronted fear in her fifties, you can too.

Stretch yourself and be willing to expand your comfort zone because life does not reward you for running away when the going gets tough.

A good understanding of a problem might show that your fear was unnecessary after all.

chapters.title: Failure only counts if you quit
chapters.text:
Life resembles learning to ride a bicycle — you fail until you learn. Persistence, repetition, enthusiasm, and supportive people sustain progress.

The only reason you'll be unable to attain success is if you quit.

Trying something new feels uncomfortable; focusing on others' opinions instead of goals makes it worse. Successful people plunge through failures as part of growth.

Michael Jordan's career shooting was about fifty percent — half his shots failed, yet he remains among the greatest.

If results lag or discouragement is frequent, check whether plans are realistic, preparation is adequate, commitment is genuine, and you love the work.

Negative people deplete positive attitude — choose company carefully.

The worst outcome is never trying. When you fail, pick yourself up and try again.

Did you know? "Chicken Soup for the Soul" was rejected by thirty-three publishers initially.

chapters.title: Build a supportive network
chapters.text:
Success indeed begins with you, but it grows through your relationships with other people. Networking — the development of relationships with people for mutual benefit — is an important life skill because you can't succeed on a large scale by yourself.

The more you help people out, the more you get what you need to achieve your goals.

Networking opens your circle to more valuable information, opportunities, and growth. But specific techniques can enhance the effectiveness of your network. They are:
• Project a winning attitude all the time. This attracts more people to you.
• Participate actively in group projects.
• Think up ways that you can serve others instead of always focusing on what is in it for you.
• Carefully refer people to others when they need help with a project.
• Care about other people and show it by calling them or listening to them when they need help.
• Make it a point to meet new people regularly and treat everyone as important.

These techniques help to build your network, but it is also up to you to follow up on your network by being appreciative of whatever help they render you. Networking takes time, so don't be surprised when it looks like you are not making any progress.

Trust that the efforts you are putting into your dream are enough to achieve success.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
It doesn't matter how complicated the project you're currently working on seems. It is possible to be successful at it. Nothing is impossible once you are determined to win at it. All you need is the right attitude and these success strategies: 1. Develop a positive attitude amid the disappointments, setbacks, and mistakes you may encounter in the course of your life. 2. Fill your mind with dominant thoughts through motivational books and audio messages that will inspire you to do better. 3. Create a mental picture of what you want for yourself because if you can't imagine it, you cannot achieve it. 4. Refuse to quit when the going gets tough. Be committed to whatever you set your mind to and devote your best effort to seeing it work. 5. Look out for the possibilities in your problems. Choose to dwell on the positive sides of any difficulty instead of the things that you cannot change. 6. Be honest, speak positive words to yourself and others because your words are potent and can shape your future. 7. Always give positive replies when people ask how you are doing. This boosts your energy daily. 8. Stop complaining about the things that bother you. Instead, seek possible solutions. 9. Associate with positive people that inspire you to grow. When you surround yourself with this kind of people, they push you to be a better version of yourself. 10. Confront your fears and grow through your mistakes. The more you try, the better you get at anything. So keep trying.

Try this

Create a visual aid that inspires you even when times get difficult. It could be a small card that you can carry around with you or a vision board in your home or office.

Read it till the end?
